ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via card name 'Recoome Eraser Gun', the close-up mouth-beam attack depicted (pink-white energy), white-and-orange Ginyu Force battle armor visible at shoulders, and Dragon Ball Wiki: Recoome Eraser Gun technique page. The Eraser Gun is Recoome's signature move and the art is a direct depiction of this specific technique.

trivia_bullets.text: Recoome Eraser Gun (BT1-106) is an Uncommon Yellow Extra card from Galactic Battle (BT1), the launch set of the Dragon Ball Super Card Game (July 28, 2017). As an Extra card it functions as Counter: Attack — when the Leader Card is Yellow, it negates an attack and prevents all opposing Battle Cards from attacking for that turn. Energy cost 4 (YY).
sources: House Rules Gaming / Big Orbit Cards product listings (via WebSearch)
PSA CardFacts: 2017 Dragon Ball Z Super Series 1 Galactic Battle

trivia_bullets.text: The Recoome Eraser Gun is a mouth-fired energy wave: Recoome charges pink energy in his mouth and releases it as a massive blast capable of distorting terrain. On Namek, Gohan saved Vegeta at the last instant while Krillin struck Recoome from behind — interrupting the beam and shattering most of Recoome's teeth.
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: Recoome Eraser Gun (via WebSearch)
DBZ Episode 64 'Recoome Unleashed' (via Dragon Ball Encyclopedia snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Recoome is the most physically imposing member of the Ginyu Force and its most flamboyant: he poses before every special move. In the Namek arc he single-handedly incapacitated Vegeta, Krillin, and Gohan before Goku's arrival. Goku neutralized him in a single strike — Recoome's defeat serving as the power-level benchmark for Goku's Namek-trained strength.
sources: TV Tropes: Dragon Ball Z Namek Arc Recap (via WebSearch)

trivia_bullets.text: The Ginyu Force fills the narrative gap between Frieza's standard henchmen and Frieza himself, functioning as a tiered power-escalation device. Recoome as the Force's 'muscle' anchors that tier. His appearance in BT1 alongside other Ginyu Force-adjacent cards makes the set a natural cohort anchor for any Ginyu Force lair.
sources: Screen Rant: Ginyu Force analysis (via WebSearch)
